task_completion.description: Open the HeLa cell Z-stack in ImageJ
task_completion.visual_evidence: An ImageJ window is open displaying an image sequence or stack, with the window title indicating it is from the 'HeLa_stack' directory.

task_completion.description: Generate a resliced cross-section of the Z-stack
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new image window in ImageJ (often titled 'Reslice...') showing a vertical cross-section (side-profile) of the cells.

task_completion.description: Measure the vertical height of a nucleus on the resliced image
task_completion.visual_evidence: A vertical straight line selection is drawn across a nucleus on the resliced image, and/or an ImageJ 'Results' window is visible showing a length/distance measurement.

task_completion.description: Save the resliced image as a TIFF file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog box, terminal command, or file manager showing the resliced image being saved as 'nucleus_reslice.tif' in the '~/ImageJ_Data/results/' directory.

task_completion.description: Save the measurement results as a CSV file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog box for the Results window, terminal command, or file manager showing the measurements being saved as 'nucleus_depth.csv' in the '~/ImageJ_Data/results/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the ImageJ application to perform the reslice and measurement
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the ImageJ GUI (menus, toolbars, image windows) rather than just writing a script or echoing text directly into a CSV file.

integrity.description: The measurement was taken along the Z-axis (vertically) on the resliced image
integrity.visual_evidence: The line drawn for measurement on the resliced image must be vertical (top to bottom), representing the Z-axis depth, rather than horizontal or drawn on the original XY plane image.

integrity.description: The recorded measurement is biologically plausible for a HeLa nucleus
integrity.visual_evidence: The length value shown in the ImageJ Results table or the saved CSV file should be between 5 and 60 (representing pixels or microns).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output files are /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/nucleus_depth.csv and /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/nucleus_reslice.tif. A valid Z-depth measurement for a HeLa nucleus in this dataset should be between 5 and 60 pixels (or microns, as the pixel spacing is 1.0).
